title: Chamaecrista olesiphylla
taxonomy: Chamaecrista olesiphylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is further classified as a member of the subgenus Chamaecrista. Chamaecrista olesiphylla is a part of the genus Chamaecrista, which includes various species of plants found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Chamaecrista olesiphylla is also supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Chamaecrista olesiphylla, also known as Indian plantain or Partridge pea, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to various parts of the world, including Africa and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
